perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall register all employees or others having gained insight into the Information. The register must be accessible to the Inventor/Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall register all employees or others having gained insight into the Information. The register will be kept internal and need not be accessible to the Inventor/Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the accessibility of the register of individuals with access to the Information. Originally, the register had to be accessible to the Inventor/Disclosing Party. The modified text states the register will be kept internal and not accessible. This in-text contradiction affects the Inventor/Disclosing Party's ability to monitor and verify who has access to their confidential Information and monitor who has acess to the information, causing contradiction on the access of information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall register all employees or others having gained insight into the Information. The register will be kept internal and need not be accessible to the Inventor/Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'The register must be accessible to the Inventor/Disclosing Party.' The changed text directly contradicts this by stating 'The register will be kept internal and need not be accessible to the Inventor/Disclosing Party.' The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, showing this in-text contradiction regarding the access of information, making the answer 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: At any time, the Inventor/Disclosing Party is entitled to be informed about how the Receiving Party keeps the Information conﬁdential in relation to third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The Inventor/Disclosing Party is not entitled to request information about the Receiving Party's confidentiality measures concerning third parties.
perturbation.explanation: This creates a direct contradiction to the receiving parties measures being conducted on third parties. The original text gives the Inventor/Disclosing Party the right to request this information. The modified text explicitly denies this right. This in-text contradiction impacts the Inventor/Disclosing Party's ability to monitor and enforce confidentiality, creating dispute with the receiving party concerning third parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Inventor/Disclosing Party is not entitled to request information about the Receiving Party's confidentiality measures concerning third parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, as the changed text explicitly denies the Inventor/Disclosing Party the right to request information about the Receiving Party's confidentiality measures concerning third parties, which is exactly what the contradicted text states. The original text grants this right, while the changed text removes it, creating a clear and irreconcilable in-text contradiction as defined by the perturbation explanation and inconsistencies definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party is held liable to the Inventor/Disclosing Party for damages in the event of breach of this Agreement. The Receiving Party is also held liable in case of breach of the Agreement by third parties to whom the Receiving Party has handed over the Information wholly or partly.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is only liable to the Inventor/Disclosing Party for direct damages demonstrably caused by its own gross negligence or willful misconduct. The Receiving Party is not liable for breaches by third parties, even if the Receiving Party handed over the information.
perturbation.explanation: This significantly alters the liability of the Receiving Party, creating a conflict. The original text makes them liable for any breach, including those by third parties. The modification limits liability to direct damages caused by gross negligence or willful misconduct, and excludes liability for third-party breaches. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty and limits the potential for legal pursuit, due to the exclusion of any liability for actions conducted by third parties, that the receiving party hands over information too.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party is only liable to the Inventor/Disclosing Party for direct damages demonstrably caused by its own gross negligence or willful misconduct. The Receiving Party is not liable for breaches by third parties, even if the Receiving Party handed over the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text makes the Receiving Party liable for *any* breach, including those by third parties. The changed text limits liability to direct damages demonstrably caused by its own gross negligence or willful misconduct and explicitly excludes liability for third-party breaches. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means it contradicts the original text. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
